q: No sooner did she get home from school than she started cooking.
a: As soon as she got home from school, she started cooking.

q: No sooner did she step out of the car than it began to rain.
a: As soon as she stepped out of the car, it began to rain.

q: No sooner had he taken the poison than he died at once.
a: As soon as he had taken the poison, he died at once.

q: No sooner did she get the newspaper than she looked at the advertisement column.
a: As soon as she got the newspaper, she looked at the advertisement column.

q: No sooner did their father go out than they switched on the television.
a: As soon as their father went out, they switched on the television.

q: No sooner did the match start than Win Thiha scored a beautiful goal.
a: As soon as the match started, Win Thiha scored a beautiful goal.

q: No sooner did Ko Ko see the visitors than he left the house by the back door.
a: As soon as Ko Ko saw the visitors, he left the house by the back door.

q: No sooner did Nyo Gyi realize that the house was on fire than he raised the alarm.
a: As soon as Nyo Gyi realized that the house was on fire, he raised the alarm.

q: No sooner did she take the medicine than her pain lessened.
a: As soon as she took the medicine, her pain lessened.

q: No sooner had I taken the medicine than I felt better.
a: As soon as I had taken the medicine, I felt better.

q: No sooner did phay phay leave the room than we started teasing May May.
a: As soon as phay phay left the room, we started teasing May May.

q: No sooner did he inherit some money than he bought a flat for his family.
a: As soon as he inherited some money, he bought a flat for his family.

q: No sooner did the dean leave the room than all the students started to argue loudly.
a: As soon as the dean left the room, all the students started to argue loudly.

q: No sooner did the Principal start to speak than the students became silent.
a: As soon as the Principal started to speak, the students became silent.

q: No sooner did she hear the music than she started dancing.
a: As soon as she heard the music, she started dancing.

q: No sooner did she get better than she did the housework.
a: As soon as she gets better, she did the housework.

q: No sooner did Mg Mg matriculate than he joined the army.
a: As soon as Mg Mg matriculated, he joined the army.

q: No sooner did they step out of the bus than it began to rain.
a: As soon as they stepped out of the bus, it began to rain.

q: No sooner did he stop smoking than his health got better.
a: As soon as he stopped smoking, his health got better.

q: No sooner had Ni Ni graduated than she got married.
a: As soon as Ni Ni had graduated, she got married.

q: No sooner did she arrive home than she had a good bath.
a: As soon as she arrived home, she had a good bath.

q: No sooner had she graduated than she got a job.
a: As soon as she had graduated, she got a job.

q: No sooner did Ko Ko enter the room than he switched on the light.
a: As soon as Ko Ko entered the room, he switched on the light.

q: No sooner did he cross the road than the traffic light turned red.
a: As soon as he crossed the road, the traffic light turned red.

q: No sooner did I sit down to study than the lights went out.
a: As soon as I sat down to study, the lights went out.

q: No sooner did he see the visitors than he left the house by the back door.
a: As soon as he saw the visitors, he left the house by the back door.

q: No sooner did he reach home than he helped his mother.
a: As soon as he reached home, he helped his mother.

q: No sooner did they step out of the bus than it began to rain.
a: As soon as they stepped out of the bus, it began to rain.

q: No sooner had I taken a bath than I felt better.
a: As soon as I had taken a bath, I felt better.
